description_long: This house features a robust stone tower with crenellated battlements, reminiscent of medieval fortifications, rising above a timber-framed lower structure. The lower section displays a classic Tudor-style facade with exposed wooden beams, white plaster infill, and a central wooden door flanked by multi-pane windows. The upper stone portion is punctuated by small, barred windows arranged in symmetrical rows, suggesting a defensive or fortified function. The roof of the tower is flat and surrounded by high parapet walls, while the attached house section has a steeply pitched, shingled gable roof. The structure sits on a raised stone platform, further enhancing its imposing presence. Architectural details such as the arched stone entryway and the mix of materials evoke a blend of residential and defensive design.
description: A fortified stone tower with crenellated battlements rises above a Tudor-style timber-framed house, featuring barred windows and a steep gable roof on a raised stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view shows the timber-framed facade with a central wooden door, multi-pane windows, and the imposing stone tower above.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the length of the stone tower, symmetrical barred windows, and the gable roof of the attached house section.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the solid stone tower with rows of barred windows and the flat, parapet-surrounded roof, with no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the stone entryway with an arched door, more barred windows, and the side of the timber-framed house.
attribute_architectural_style: Tudor Revival with Medieval Fortified Tower
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows of small barred windows on the tower, regular multi-pane windows on the house
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with stone arch, no porch, and a fortified gate on the tower section
